title: Permutations of Distinct Elements
description:
Given Generate all permutations of a list of distinct integers. Use a visited set or in-place swapping. Produce permutations in any order.

    

Constraints:
    - 0 ≤ n ≤ 9
- All elements distinct
explanation:
Intuition
Each position can take any remaining element. Backtracking explores the n choices at the first position, then n-1 at the next, and so on.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Use a used[] boolean list
2. Main Algorithm: Append unused element, mark used, recurse, then unmark and pop
3. Return Result: When path length equals n, add to result

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(n·n!)
- Space Complexity: O(n)

Key Insights
- Choice, explore, un-choose pattern
- Visit flags prevent duplicates

Common Pitfalls
- Forgetting to unmark used leads to missing candidates
- Reusing the same list reference in results
solutionCode:
def permute(nums):
    n = len(nums)
    used = [False] * n
    res, path = [], []
    def dfs():
        if len(path) == n:
            res.append(path[:])
            return
        for i in range(n):
            if used[i]:
                continue
            used[i] = True
            path.append(nums[i])
            dfs()
            path.pop()
            used[i] = False
    dfs()
    return res
